alternative_parents.description: Polyhydroxylated oxosteroids possessing a cyclopentano-perhydro-phenantrene with beta side-chain at C-17, which is the product of terpene biosynthesis through mevalonic acid, cholesterol and related sterols. Ecdysteroids are found in certain plants and animals.
alternative_parents.name: Ecdysteroids

alternative_parents.description: Steroid derivatives carrying a C=O group at the 6-position of the steroid skeleton.
alternative_parents.name: 6-oxosteroids

alternative_parents.description: 3-hydroxy steroids containing a double bond between positions 7 and 8.
alternative_parents.name: 3-hydroxy delta-7-steroids

alternative_parents.description: Steroids carrying a beta-hydroxyl group at the 3-position of the steroid backbone.
alternative_parents.name: 3-beta-hydroxysteroids

alternative_parents.description: Steroids carrying a hydroxyl group at the 14-position of the steroid backbone.
alternative_parents.name: 14-hydroxysteroids

alternative_parents.description: Steroids containing a double bond between positions 7 and 8.
alternative_parents.name: Delta-7-steroids

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a cylohexenone moiety, which is a six-membered aliphatic ring that carries a ketone and has one endocyclic double bond.
alternative_parents.name: Cyclohexenones

alternative_parents.description: Compounds in which a hydroxy group, -OH, is attached to a saturated carbon atom R3COH (R not H ).
alternative_parents.name: Tertiary alcohols

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a secondary alcohol functional group, with the general structure HOC(R)(R') (R,R'=alkyl, aryl).
alternative_parents.name: Secondary alcohols

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an aliphatic ring substituted with at least one hydroxyl group.
alternative_parents.name: Cyclic alcohols and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing more than one hydroxyl groups.
alternative_parents.name: Polyols

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an oxide group.
alternative_parents.name: Organic oxides

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of hydrocarbons obtained by substituting one or more carbon atoms by an heteroatom. They contain at least one carbon atom and heteroatom.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrocarbon derivatives

ancestors: Hydroxy bile acids, alcohols and derivatives
Pentahydroxy bile acids, alcohols and derivatives

class.description: Compounds based on the cyclopenta[a]phenanthrene carbon skeleton, partially or completely hydrogenated; there are usually methyl groups at C-10 and C-13, and often an alkyl group at C-17. By extension, one or more bond scissions, ring expansions and/or ring contractions of the skeleton may have occurred.
class.name: Steroids and steroid derivatives

description: This compound belongs to the class of organic compounds known as pentahydroxy bile acids, alcohols and derivatives. These are bile acids, alcohols or derivatives bearing five hydroxyl groups.

direct_parent.description: Bile acids, alcohols or derivatives bearing five hydroxyl groups.
direct_parent.name: Pentahydroxy bile acids, alcohols and derivatives

intermediate_nodes.description: Bile acids, alcohols or derivatives bearing at least hydroxyl group.
intermediate_nodes.name: Hydroxy bile acids, alcohols and derivatives

kingdom.description: Compounds that contain at least one carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Organic compounds

subclass.description: Organic compounds containing an alcohol or acid derivative of cholic acid.
subclass.name: Bile acids, alcohols and derivatives

substituents: Pentahydroxy bile acid, alcohol, or derivatives

superclass.description: Fatty acids and their derivatives, and substances related biosynthetically or functionally to these compounds.
superclass.name: Lipids and lipid-like molecules
